highlightedText: and list 6 important “travel tips” about Copenhagen.

text: and list 6 important “travel tips” about Copenhagen.
The type of power sockets used in Denmark are Type A and Type K. It's essential to carry a travel adapter if your devices use different plug types.
The official currency in Denmark is the Danish Krone (DKK). Most places do not accept euros, so it's better to have some local currency on hand.
Copenhagen has a total of 6 metro lines that can take you anywhere in the city. It's a convenient and efficient way to travel around.
The weather in Copenhagen in December can get quite chilly, with temperatures dropping below -15 degrees Celsius. Be sure to pack warm clothing.
The official language is Danish, but English is widely spoken in Copenhagen. However, it's always appreciated if you learn a few basic Danish phrases.
You can get a Copenhagen Card that covers 80 attractions and public transports with $30. It's a great way to save money if you plan to visit several sights.
